Beast (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $31,846,530Details
International Box Office $27,321,809
Worldwide Box Office $59,168,339
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,390,424
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,626,979
Total $3,017,403
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Dr. Nate Samuels is a recently widowed husband who returns to South Africa, where he first met his wife, on a long-planned trip with their daughters to a game reserve. But what begins as a journey of healing jolts into a fearsome fight for survival when a lion, a survivor of blood-thirsty poachers who now sees all humans as the enemy, begins stalking them.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,575,855 (36.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.75 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$36,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,743 opening theaters/3,754 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $36,171,370

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 19th, 2022 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: August 10th, 2022 (Wide) (Egypt)
August 10th, 2022 (Wide) (Belgium)
August 11th, 2022 (Wide) (Bolivia)
August 11th, 2022 (Wide) (Malaysia)
August 11th, 2022 (Wide) (Uruguay)
Video Release: October 4th, 2022 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violent content, bloody images and some language.
(Rating bulletin 2738 (cert #53855), 6/29/2022)
Running Time: 93 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Filming location (country): Namibia, South Africa
Geographical setting: South Africa
Plot point: Animals Gone Bad, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Family Vacations, Tourists In Trouble, Widow/Widower, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Subgenre: Survival Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Will Packer Productions, Universal Pictures, RVK
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Dr. Nate Samuels is a recently widowed husband who returns to South Africa, where he first met his wife, on a long-planned trip with their daughters to a game reserve.
